hacking.notes: Unauthorized third party used a compromised (stolen) user account to access Lifepoint Health's internal environment and certain internal databases.

name: Unknown
external.notes: Described in all sources only as an 'unauthorized third party'; no group named.

confidentiality.notes: Names, addresses, phone numbers, dates of birth and Social Security numbers of employees of contracted vendors. Coded 'Yes' per skill rule: confirmed unauthorized access to live databases holding the data. Exact record count not disclosed.

plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23384
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260702T132320Z

Lifepoint Health, Inc. (Brentwood, TN; operates 60+ hospital campuses in 28 states plus 30+ rehab/behavioral hospitals). On 2026-02-22 an unauthorized third party used a compromised user account to gain access to the internal environment and certain internal databases; discovered next day 2026-02-23 and contained within 24 hours. Notifications began 2026-04-23. Exposed data (names, addresses, phone numbers, DOB, SSNs) belonged to employees of contracted vendors only — not patients or direct employees, so no Medical data variety coded. SSNs -> 'Sensitive Personal', remaining PII -> 'Personal'.

Assumptions / Unknowns: actor.external.variety and name = Unknown (sources say only 'unauthorized third party', no group claimed/named). actor.motive = 'Financial' is an INFERENCE from the targeting of SSN/PII via stolen creds — no exfiltration, ransom, or extortion was reported. hacking.vector = Unknown (sources do not state how the account was compromised or the access path). discovery_method.internal.variety = Unknown (victim detected it internally the next day but the detecting control is unstated). confidentiality.state = 'Stored' default (encryption status not stated). asset hosting coded On-Premise from 'internal environment/internal databases' wording. Total affected count NOT disclosed anywhere, so data_total omitted rather than logging a partial figure.

NAICS: issue label is 'NAICS 62: Health Care and Social Assistance'; the issue body suggested 621111 (Offices of Physicians), but Lifepoint Health is a hospital operator, so coded 622110 (General Medical and Surgical Hospitals) as more accurate.

PHIDBR2019 label IGNORED per skill (artifact tag; its '2019' is not the incident year — real incident date established as Feb 2026 from sources).

Sources consulted: issue body (claimdepot.com/data-breach/lifepoint-health-2026); HIPAA Journal (hipaajournal.com, 2026-06-25 comment); databreachclassaction.io (2026-06-26 comment); plus independent corroboration — Behavioral Healthcare Network republication (bhnet.org, 2026-06-23) and the NEWER independent source Strauss Borrelli PLLC data-breach blog (straussborrelli.com/data-breach-blog, listing last updated 2026-07-01) confirming the law-firm investigation. The newer source did NOT change or contradict any facts.

event_chain.summary: Use of stolen creds (compromised user account) to access internal environment
Unauthorized access to internal databases holding vendor-employee PII/SSNs

summary: Lifepoint Health, Inc. disclosed that on Feb 22, 2026 an unauthorized third party used a compromised (stolen) user account to gain access to its internal environment and certain internal databases. The activity was detected the next day and contained within 24 hours. The exposed databases held names, addresses, phone numbers, dates of birth and Social Security numbers of employees of contracted vendors (not patients or direct employees). Affected individuals were notified beginning April 23, 2026 and offered credit monitoring.
